title: Getting started with genhaz
text: survival analysis, covariate effects typically modelled one two mechanisms: multiplicative effect hazard (proportional hazards, PH) multiplicative effect time (accelerated failure time, AFT). Generalized hazard (GH) models combine simultaneously: h(t∣x)=h0(teβ1⊤x)⋅e(β1+β2)⊤xh(t \mid x) = h_0\!\left(t\,e^{\beta_1^\top x}\right) \cdot e^{(\beta_1+\beta_2)^\top x} nests PH (β1=0\beta_1 = 0), AFT (β2=0\beta_2 = 0), additive hazards (AH, β1=−β2\beta_1 = -\beta_2) special cases. key advantage: combining time-acceleration parameter β1\beta_1 hazard-scaling parameter β2\beta_2 can capture time-varying hazard ratios two parameters per covariate. genhaz fits penalised cubic restricted spline GH models log-time scale, smoothing parameter selected automatically minimising modified likelihood cross-validation (LCV) criterion.

title: Getting started with genhaz
text: model_type argument selects sub-model per covariate: Mixed models supported via vector, e.g. model_type = c("PH", "GH") two covariates.

title: Getting started with genhaz
text: Note: rc tested point time.

title: Getting started with genhaz
text: use biostat3::melanoma dataset: 7,775 patients melanoma cancer, including age group, period diagnosis, sex, cancer stage, survival time months, death indicator. question interest effect non-localised stage survival, adjusted age, period, sex.

title: Getting started with genhaz
code: library(biostat3)  mel        <- biostat3::melanoma mel$X      <- ifelse(mel$stage == "Localised", 0, 1) mel$event  <- ifelse(mel$status == "Dead: cancer", 1, 0) mel$time   <- mel$surv_mm mel$period <- ifelse(mel$year8594 == "Diagnosed 75-84", 0, 1)

title: Getting started with genhaz
text: full model fit takes approximately 9 minutes re-run . code shows exactly run produce stored result:
code: fit_melanoma <- fit_genhaz(   Surv(mel$time, mel$event), ~ X + period + agegrp + sex,   data       = mel,   model_type = "GH",   profile    = TRUE,   n_knots    = 8,   tol_LCV    = 0.001,   lcv_method = "optimize" ) library(genhaz) library(survival) data("fit_melanoma") library(biostat3) mel        <- biostat3::melanoma mel$X      <- ifelse(mel$stage == "Localised", 0, 1) mel$period <- ifelse(mel$year8594 == "Diagnosed 75-84", 0, 1) new_time   <- seq(0.5, 320, by = 0.5)   # avoids t = 0 (needed for time_ratio)  nd_mel <- data.frame(   X      = c(1L, 0L),   period = c(1L, 1L),   agegrp = factor(c("60-74", "60-74"), levels = levels(mel$agegrp)),   sex    = factor(c("Male",  "Male"),  levels = levels(mel$sex)) ) rownames(nd_mel) <- c("Non-Localised", "Localised")

title: Getting started with genhaz
text: Patients non-localised disease progress baseline hazard exp⁡(β̂1)\exp(\hat\beta_1) times faster face exp⁡(β̂2)\exp(\hat\beta_2) times higher hazard every time point. Wald CIs :

title: Getting started with genhaz
text: plot() dispatches fitted model, calls predict() internally, sets ylim full CI range confidence bands never clipped. Evaluated age group 60–74, male sex, diagnosed 1985–94.
code: plot(fit_melanoma, newdata = nd_mel, times = new_time, type = "hazard",      interval = "confidence", col = c("steelblue", "firebrick"),      xlab = "Time (months)", main = "Estimated hazard — melanoma, GH model") plot(fit_melanoma, newdata = nd_mel, times = new_time, type = "survival",      interval = "confidence", col = c("steelblue", "firebrick"),      xlab = "Time (months)", main = "Estimated survival — melanoma, GH model")

title: Getting started with genhaz
text: type = "hazard_ratio" gives h1(t)/h0(t)h_1(t)/h_0(t) (exposed baseline) log-scale delta-method CI. PH flat; GH model captures time variation. plot() works directly predict() result.  two parameters stage effect, GH model captures time-varying hazard ratio: high diagnosis, levelling approximately 6 years.
code: hr_mel <- predict(fit_melanoma, newdata = nd_mel,                   times = seq(0.5, 200, by = 0.5), type = "hazard_ratio",                   interval = "confidence") plot(hr_mel, col = "purple",      xlab = "Time (months)",      main = "Time-varying HR — non-localised vs localised") abline(h = 1, lty = 2, col = "grey50")

title: Getting started with genhaz
text: type = "surv_diff" gives S1(t)−S0(t)S_1(t) - S_0(t) (exposed minus baseline) linear scale; delta-method accounts correlation two curves. type = "rmst_diff" integrates difference restriction time τ\tau via 25-point Gauss-Legendre quadrature.
code: diff_s_mel <- predict(fit_melanoma, newdata = nd_mel,                       times = new_time, type = "surv_diff",                       interval = "confidence") plot(diff_s_mel,      xlab = "Time (months)",      main = "S(t | Non-localised) − S(t | Localised)") abline(h = 0, lty = 3, col = "grey70") rmst_mel <- predict(fit_melanoma, newdata = nd_mel,                     times = new_time, type = "rmst_diff",                     interval = "confidence") plot(rmst_mel,      xlab = "Time (months)",      main = "RMST(t | Non-localised) − RMST(t | Localised)") abline(h = 0, lty = 3, col = "grey70")

title: Getting started with genhaz
text: type = "time_ratio" gives τ/t\tau/t S0(τ)=S1(t)S_0(\tau) = S_1(t) (relation S(t∣x1)=S0(τ(t))S(t\mid x_1)=S_0(\tau(t))): baseline time τ\tau localised (baseline, group 0, row 2) group reaches survival level non-localised (exposed, group 1, row 1) group time tt. non-localised group fares worse, localised group needs longer fall far, giving τ>t\tau > t. τ\tau found via uniroot; delta-method CI uses implicit function theorem. search capped model support exp(max(fit$knots)) (tau_max); localised baseline survival plateaus non-localised level, beyond early window τ\tau exists (returned NA) — therefore use -support grid.
code: tr_mel <- predict(fit_melanoma, newdata = nd_mel,                   times = seq(2, 24, by = 2), type = "time_ratio",                   interval = "confidence") plot(tr_mel, col = "darkorange",      xlab = "Time (months)", main = "Time ratio — non-localised vs localised") abline(h = 1, lty = 2, col = "grey50")

title: Getting started with genhaz
text: type = "acc_factor" reports time-varying acceleration effect log scale, f(t)=log⁡τ′(t)=log⁡h1(t)−log⁡h0(τ(t)),f(t) = \log \tau'(t) = \log h_1(t) - \log h_0(\tau(t)), time-varying analogue β1\beta_1: defined S(t∣x1)=S0(∫0tef(u)Xdu),S(t \mid x_1) = S_0\!\left(\int_0^t e^{f(u)\,X}\,du\right), .e. warped time τ(t)=∫0tef(u)Xdu\tau(t)=\int_0^t e^{f(u)X}du instantaneous rate ef(t)X=τ′(t)e^{f(t)X}=\tau'(t), f(t)=β1f(t)=\beta_1 constant-AFT limit. f(t)f(t) directly comparable fitted β̂1\hat\beta_1 (≈1.14\approx 1.14). delta-method CI uses implicit function theorem together hazard time-derivative post(..., "dh_dt"); support cap applies, use -support grid. work progress.
code: af_mel <- predict(fit_melanoma, newdata = nd_mel,                   times = seq(2, 24, by = 2), type = "acc_factor",                   interval = "confidence") plot(af_mel, col = "purple",      xlab = "Time (months)", main = "Acceleration effect f(t) — non-localised vs localised") abline(h = fit_melanoma$par["beta1_X"], lty = 2, col = "grey50")  # constant-AFT beta1

title: Getting started with genhaz
text: sim_scenario() generates right-censored survival data one three named scenarios mixture-Weibull baseline hazards (bathtub, hump-shaped, early peak). mixWeibSc() evaluates corresponding true hh, HH, SS grid comparison fitted estimates.
code: set.seed(82359) dat <- sim_scenario(scenario = 1, beta1 = 0.5, beta2 = 0.5, n = 2000) head(dat) #>        time X event    T_true #> 1 2.1526916 0     1 2.1526916 #> 2 3.1407413 0     1 3.1407413 #> 3 0.4359473 0     0 1.8678244 #> 4 1.5510083 1     1 1.5510083 #> 5 0.4094107 0     1 0.4094107 #> 6 1.2020419 1     1 1.2020419 fit <- fit_genhaz(   surv       = Surv(dat$time, dat$event),   formula    = ~ X,   data       = dat,   model_type = "GH",   profile    = TRUE,   n_knots    = 6,   tol_LCV    = 0.05,   lcv_method = "optimize" ) print(fit) #> Fitted generalized hazard model (genhaz) #>  #>   Model type : GH #>   Knots      : 6 (log-time scale) #>   Lambda     : 287.4 #>   EDF        : 6.91 #>   AIC        : 3521.11 #>  #> Covariate coefficients: #>         Estimate Std.Err     z Pr(>|z|)     #> beta1_X  0.45585 0.04649 9.806  < 2e-16 *** #> beta2_X  0.61353 0.11273 5.442 5.26e-08 *** #> --- #> Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1 #>  #> Exponentiated estimates (95% CI): #>         exp(Est) lower .95 upper .95 #> beta1_X   1.5775    1.4401    1.7280 #> beta2_X   1.8469    1.4808    2.3036

title: Getting started with genhaz
text: confint() gives Wald CIs parameters; diff = TRUE returns CI β1−β2\beta_1 - \beta_2 (accounting covariance). Fit restricted (PH) model test full GH model:
code: confint(fit, "beta1_X") #>             2.5 %    97.5 % #> beta1_X 0.3647416 0.5469618 confint(fit, "beta2_X") #>             2.5 %    97.5 % #> beta2_X 0.3925786 0.8344796 confint(fit, c("beta1_X", "beta2_X"), diff = TRUE) #>                        2.5 %    97.5 % #> beta1_X - beta2_X -0.4629392 0.1475844 fit_ph <- fit_genhaz(   surv       = Surv(dat$time, dat$event),   formula    = ~ X,   data       = dat,   model_type = "PH",   profile    = TRUE,   n_knots    = 6,   tol_LCV    = 0.05,   lcv_method = "optimize" )  anova(fit_ph, fit) #> Analysis of Deviance Table (likelihood ratio tests) #>  #>  Model 1: ~X, PH #>  Model 2: ~X, GH #>         Df  LogLik  Chisq Chi Df Pr(>Chisq)     #> Model 1  7 -1790.4                              #> Model 2  8 -1756.6 67.608      1  < 2.2e-16 *** #> --- #> Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1

title: Getting started with genhaz
text: smoothing parameter λ\lambda chosen minimising modified LCV criterion. Three strategies available via lcv_method:
code: # First-order gradient (faster) fit <- fit_genhaz(..., profile = TRUE, lcv_method = "approx")  # Pure optimisation (no gradient) fit <- fit_genhaz(..., profile = TRUE, lcv_method = "optimize")

what: genhaz — Penalised Flexible-Parametric Generalised Hazard Models
title: genhaz — Penalised Flexible-Parametric Generalised Hazard Models
text: Penalised Flexible-Parametric Generalized Hazard Models Survival Analysis survival analysis, quantifying covariate effects typically relies one two complementary frameworks. Cox proportional hazards (PH) model acts multiplicatively instantaneous hazard — h(t|x) = h₀(t)·exp(β′x) — requires covariate effect remain constant time, assumption often difficult justify practice. Accelerated failure time (AFT) models instead model covariate effects time scale, describing survival accelerated decelerated version baseline — h(t|x) = h₀(t·exp(β′x))·exp(β′x). lesser-known accelerated hazards (AH) models describe pure time-acceleration accompanying hazard-scale change: h(t|x) = h₀(t·exp(β′x)). fundamental reason covariate effect restricted one scales. Generalized hazards (GH) models (also “extended hazards”) combine mechanisms: nests PH (β₁ = 0), AFT (β₂ = 0), AH (β₁ = −β₂) special cases. key property: combining time-constant acceleration parameter β₁ time-constant hazard-scaling parameter β₂ may allow GH model capture time-varying hazard ratios two parameters per covariate. Despite attractive properties, flexible parametric implementation GH models regularisation avoid overfitting previously existed. genhaz fills gap: fits penalised cubic restricted spline GH models log-time baseline, smoothing parameter selected automatically minimising modified likelihood cross-validation (LCV) criterion. Simulation studies showed log-time spline formulation critical accurately capturing rapidly varying early hazards. Link paper published.
code: h(t|x) = h₀(t·exp(β₁′x)) · exp((β₁+β₂)′x)

title: genhaz — Penalised Flexible-Parametric Generalised Hazard Models
text: Required system tools: C++ compiler Rtools toolchain (Windows) Xcode command-line tools (macOS).
code: # From source (inside the package directory): devtools::install(".")  # Or build and install the tarball: devtools::build() install.packages("genhaz_0.1.0.tar.gz", repos = NULL, type = "source")

title: genhaz — Penalised Flexible-Parametric Generalised Hazard Models
text: Optional: survPen (lambda_surv = TRUE comparison plots), rstpm2 (translate_time()), biostat3 (melanoma example ).

title: genhaz — Penalised Flexible-Parametric Generalised Hazard Models
code: library(genhaz) library(survival)  set.seed(42) dat <- sim_scenario(scenario = 1, beta1 = 0.5, beta2 = 0.5, n = 2000)  # Fit a GH model with automatic smoothing (LCV) fit <- fit_genhaz(   surv       = Surv(dat$time, dat$event),   formula    = ~ X,   data       = dat,   model_type = "GH",   profile    = TRUE,   # optimise lambda via LCV   n_knots    = 8,   tol_LCV    = 0.01 )  fit$par fit$se  # Hazard and survival curves with 95 % confidence bands t_grid <- seq(0.01, 8, length.out = 300) nd     <- data.frame(X = c(0, 1)) rownames(nd) <- c("X = 0", "X = 1")  plot(fit, newdata = nd, times = t_grid, type = "hazard",      interval = "confidence", col = c("steelblue", "firebrick")) plot(fit, newdata = nd, times = t_grid, type = "survival",      interval = "confidence", col = c("steelblue", "firebrick"))

title: genhaz — Penalised Flexible-Parametric Generalised Hazard Models
text: objects returned fit_genhaz() class "genhaz_fit" come standard R generics. Inspect fit Predict hazard, survival, cumulative hazard, RMST time grid — one row newdata per covariate pattern, row names become group labels: Following stats::predict.lm, interval = "none" (default) returns point estimate (lower/upper NA); pass interval = "confidence" delta-method Wald CI, level setting confidence level (default 0.95). five two-group types require exactly two rows newdata: row 1 exposed group (group 1) row 2 unexposed/baseline group (group 0). Results labelled "group1 - group0"; request CIs interval = "confidence": acc_factor work progress Plot automatic colours; add interval = "confidence" delta-method confidence bands:
code: print(fit)        # model type, lambda, EDF, AIC, coefficient table with Wald CIs summary(fit)      # as above plus exponentiated estimates exp(beta1), exp(beta2) nd <- data.frame(X = c(1, 0))            # row 1 = exposed (group 1), row 2 = baseline (group 0) rownames(nd) <- c("Exposed", "Unexposed")  pred <- predict(fit, newdata = nd, times = t_grid, type = "survival",                 interval = "confidence") # type = "hazard" | "survival" | "cumhaz" | "rmst" #      | "surv_diff" | "rmst_diff" | "hazard_ratio" | "time_ratio" | "acc_factor" # returns a data.frame: pattern | time | estimate | lower | upper tau_grid <- c(2, 4, 6, 8)  predict(fit, newdata = nd, times = t_grid,  type = "surv_diff")    # S1(t) - S0(t) predict(fit, newdata = nd, times = tau_grid, type = "rmst_diff")   # RMST1 - RMST0 predict(fit, newdata = nd, times = t_grid,  type = "hazard_ratio") # h1(t) / h0(t) predict(fit, newdata = nd, times = t_grid,  type = "time_ratio")   # tau/t: S0(tau)=S1(t) predict(fit, newdata = nd, times = t_grid,  type = "acc_factor")   # f(t)=log tau'(t): S(t|x)=S0(int exp(f*X)) plot(fit, newdata = nd, times = t_grid)                  # hazard (default), no bands plot(fit, newdata = nd, times = t_grid, type = "survival", interval = "confidence") plot(fit, newdata = nd, times = t_grid, col = c("steelblue", "firebrick"))

title: genhaz — Penalised Flexible-Parametric Generalised Hazard Models
text: model_type argument selects sub-model covariate: Mixed models supported via vector, e.g. model_type = c("PH", "GH") two covariates.

title: genhaz — Penalised Flexible-Parametric Generalised Hazard Models
text: Note: right-censoring type properly tested far.

title: genhaz — Penalised Flexible-Parametric Generalised Hazard Models
text: use biostat3::melanoma dataset, synthetic dataset mimicking melanoma cancer patient survival Nordic population. contains 3,680 female 4,095 male patients together age, year diagnosis, stage cancer progression (localised, regional, distant, unknown), observed survival time months, end--follow-status indicator. question interest effect non-localised cancer stage diagnosis survival. Age (grouped 0–44, 45–59, 60–74, 75+), period diagnosis (1975–84 vs 1985–94), sex treated confounders.

title: genhaz — Penalised Flexible-Parametric Generalised Hazard Models
text: GH model application X = (1[period=1985–94], 1[age∈45–59], 1[age∈60–74], 1[age≥75], 1[sex=male])’.
code: h(t | age, period, sex, stage) =     h₀( t · exp(β₁_stage · 1[stage≠loc] + β₁_con' X) )     · exp( (β₁_stage + β₂_stage) · 1[stage≠loc] + (β₁_con + β₂_con)' X )

title: genhaz — Penalised Flexible-Parametric Generalised Hazard Models
code: library(genhaz) library(survival) library(biostat3)  set.seed(23234)  mel        <- biostat3::melanoma mel$X      <- ifelse(mel$stage == "Localised", 0, 1) mel$event  <- ifelse(mel$status == "Dead: cancer", 1, 0) mel$time   <- mel$surv_mm mel$period <- ifelse(mel$year8594 == "Diagnosed 75-84", 0, 1)  fit_adj <- fit_genhaz(   Surv(mel$time, mel$event), ~ X + period + agegrp + sex,   data       = mel,   model_type = "GH",   profile    = TRUE,   n_knots    = 8,   tol_LCV    = 0.001,   timeIt     = TRUE,   lcv_method = "optimize" )  fit_adj$par fit_adj$se

title: genhaz — Penalised Flexible-Parametric Generalised Hazard Models
text: Delta-method confidence intervals via predict(). Results evaluated age group 60–74, male sex, diagnosed 1985–94. Plots also overlay estimates survPen (dashed lines) comparison.
code: nd_mel <- data.frame(   X      = c(0L, 1L),   period = c(1L, 1L),   agegrp = factor(c("60-74", "60-74"), levels = levels(mel$agegrp)),   sex    = factor(c("Male",  "Male"),  levels = levels(mel$sex)) ) rownames(nd_mel) <- c("Localised", "Non-localised") new.time <- seq(0.5, max(mel$time), by = 0.5)

title: genhaz — Penalised Flexible-Parametric Generalised Hazard Models
text: Since GH model imposes common baseline hazard shape starting 0, slight misspecification early times; negligible considering overall survival curves (see ). estimates stage effect β̂₁ = 1.14 (95% CI: 0.97, 1.31) β̂₂ = 0.31 (95% CI: 0.17, 0.44). Patients non-localised disease progress baseline hazard exp(1.14) = 3.14 times faster experience exp(0.31) = 1.36 times higher hazard every time point.

title: genhaz — Penalised Flexible-Parametric Generalised Hazard Models
code: plot(fit_adj, newdata = nd_mel, times = new.time, type = "survival",      interval = "confidence", col = c("steelblue", "firebrick"),      xlab = "Time (months)", main = "Estimated survival — melanoma, GH model")

title: genhaz — Penalised Flexible-Parametric Generalised Hazard Models
text: two parameters (β₁, β₂) stage effect, GH model captures time-variation hazard ratio recovered survPen fully flexibly. models agree hazard ratio starts high levels approximately 6 years.
code: hr_mel <- predict(fit_adj, newdata = nd_mel,                   times = seq(0.5, 200, by = 0.5), type = "hazard_ratio",                   interval = "confidence") plot(hr_mel, col = "purple",      xlab = "Time (months)",      main = "Time-varying HR — non-localised vs localised") abline(h = 1, lty = 2, col = "grey50")

title: genhaz — Penalised Flexible-Parametric Generalised Hazard Models
text: genhaz ships three mixture Weibull baseline scenarios qualitatively different hazard shapes benchmarking. true baseline hazard follows two-component Weibull mixture: h0(t)=pλ1γ1tγ1−1e−λ1tγ1+(1−p)λ2γ2tγ2−1e−λ2tγ2pe−λ1tγ1+(1−p)e−λ2tγ2h_0(t) = \frac{p\,\lambda_1\gamma_1 t^{\gamma_1-1}e^{-\lambda_1 t^{\gamma_1}} + (1-p)\,\lambda_2\gamma_2 t^{\gamma_2-1}e^{-\lambda_2 t^{\gamma_2}}}{p\,e^{-\lambda_1 t^{\gamma_1}}+(1-p)\,e^{-\lambda_2 t^{\gamma_2}}} covariate effect enters generalised hazard: h(t|X) = h₀(t · exp(β₁X)) · exp((β₁ + β₂)X).
code: # Simulate from scenario 1 (bathtub baseline) dat1 <- sim_scenario(1, beta1 = 0.5, beta2 = 0.5, n = 1000)  # Evaluate the true hazard on a grid t_grid <- seq(0.01, 8, length.out = 300) h_true <- mixWeibSc(1, "h", t_grid, X = 0, beta1 = 0.5, beta2 = 0.5)

title: genhaz — Penalised Flexible-Parametric Generalised Hazard Models
text: smoothing parameter λ chosen minimising modified LCV criterion. Three optimisation strategies available via lcv_method argument:
code: # Faster first-order gradient fit <- fit_genhaz(..., profile = TRUE, lcv_method = "approx")  # Pure optimisation (no gradient) fit <- fit_genhaz(..., profile = TRUE, lcv_method = "optimize")

title: genhaz — Penalised Flexible-Parametric Generalised Hazard Models

what: Known issues / R CMD check notes
title: genhaz — Penalised Flexible-Parametric Generalised Hazard Models
text: getFromNamespace("vintegrate", "rstpm2") gaussified = FALSE code path triggers R CMD check NOTE. path default; Gauss-Legendre path (gaussified = TRUE) recommended. translate_time() translate_time2() require rstpm2 (>= 1.5).

what: Pointwise confidence bands for hazard and survival functions — CI
title: Pointwise confidence bands for hazard and survival functions — CI
text: Computes pointwise 95 % (level) confidence intervals cumulative hazard H(t), survival S(t) = exp(-H(t)), hazard h(t), given covariate pattern, using delta method log scale.

title: Pointwise confidence bands for hazard and survival functions — CI
code: CI(fit, t, covariate, alpha = 0.05)

title: Pointwise confidence bands for hazard and survival functions — CI
text: fit fitted model object fit_genhaz(). t Numeric vector evaluation time points. covariate Numeric vector covariate values (one per covariate model). alpha Significance level. Default 0.05 (95 % CI).

title: Pointwise confidence bands for hazard and survival functions — CI
text: data frame columns time, H, lower_H, upper_H, S, lower_S, upper_S, h, lower_h, upper_h.

title: Pointwise confidence bands for hazard and survival functions — CI
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ t_grid <- seq(0.01, 8, length.out = 200) ci     <- CI(fit, t_grid, covariate = 0) plot(ci$time, ci$h, type = "l") lines(ci$time, ci$lower_h, lty = 2) lines(ci$time, ci$upper_h, lty = 2) } # }

what: Likelihood ratio tests between nested GH models — anova.genhaz_fit
title: Likelihood ratio tests between nested GH models — anova.genhaz_fit
text: Compares two nested "genhaz_fit" models chi-squared likelihood-ratio tests, style stats::anova() lm/glm objects. Models compared sequentially order supplied, list restricted model first.

title: Likelihood ratio tests between nested GH models — anova.genhaz_fit
code: # S3 method for class 'genhaz_fit' anova(object, ...)

title: Likelihood ratio tests between nested GH models — anova.genhaz_fit
text: object "genhaz_fit" object fit_genhaz() (first, usually restricted, model). ... One "genhaz_fit" objects compare object. Non-"genhaz_fit" arguments ignored.

title: Likelihood ratio tests between nested GH models — anova.genhaz_fit
text: analysis--deviance table class c("anova", "data.frame") one row per model columns Df (model degrees freedom), LogLik, Chisq, Chi Df, Pr(>Chisq) (comparison columns NA first model).

title: Likelihood ratio tests between nested GH models — anova.genhaz_fit
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ fit_ph <- fit_genhaz(Surv(dat$time, dat$event), ~X,                      data = dat, model_type = "PH", n_knots = 6) fit_gh <- fit_genhaz(Surv(dat$time, dat$event), ~X,                      data = dat, model_type = "GH", n_knots = 6) anova(fit_ph, fit_gh) } # }

what: Wald confidence intervals for model parameters — confint.genhaz_fit
title: Wald confidence intervals for model parameters — confint.genhaz_fit
text: Computes Wald confidence intervals parameters fitted GH model, following stats::confint() convention. diff = TRUE instead returns delta-method interval difference two parameters, accounting covariance.

title: Wald confidence intervals for model parameters — confint.genhaz_fit
code: # S3 method for class 'genhaz_fit' confint(object, parm, level = 0.95, diff = FALSE, ...)

title: Wald confidence intervals for model parameters — confint.genhaz_fit
text: object "genhaz_fit" object fit_genhaz(). parm Character vector parameter names (must object$parnames). missing, parameters used. diff = TRUE, exactly two names must supplied. level Confidence level. Default 0.95. diff Logical. TRUE, return single interval parm[1] - parm[2] via delta method. Default FALSE. ... Currently unused.

title: Wald confidence intervals for model parameters — confint.genhaz_fit
text: matrix one row per parameter (single row difference) two columns giving lower upper bounds.

title: Wald confidence intervals for model parameters — confint.genhaz_fit
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ confint(fit)                                       # all parameters confint(fit, "beta2_X")                            # one parameter confint(fit, c("beta1_X", "beta2_X"), diff = TRUE) # difference } # }

what: C++ reimplementation of survPen::crs, including derivatives. — crs_cpp
title: C++ reimplementation of survPen::crs, including derivatives. — crs_cpp
text: re-implementation uses algorithm uses different defaults, allows derivatives. changes defaults: function assumes intercept false (per splines::ns); knots specified, function defaults using x rather unique(x) quantiles; df argument comes knots argument. use survPen::crs defaults R, use crs_cpp(..., intercept=TRUE, survPen_compatible=TRUE).

title: C++ reimplementation of survPen::crs, including derivatives. — crs_cpp
code: crs_cpp(   x,   df = 10L,   knots = NULL,   derivs = 0L,   intercept = FALSE,   survPen_compatible = FALSE )

title: C++ reimplementation of survPen::crs, including derivatives. — crs_cpp
text: x vec values provide df non-negative integer degrees freedom (ignored knots specified) knots vec knots (defaults NULL) derivs non-negative integer derivative (default 0) intercept bool whether include intercept survPen_compatible bool whether use unique(x) determining knots knots specified

title: C++ reimplementation of survPen::crs, including derivatives. — crs_cpp
text: Rcpp::NumericMatrix attributes class="crs_cpp", pen penalty matrix, knots knots, derivs order derivatives, intercept bool/logical whether intercept included.

title: C++ reimplementation of survPen::crs, including derivatives. — crs_cpp
code: x     <- seq(0, 1, length.out = 11) knots <- c(1, 3, 5, 7) / 10 crs1  <- crs_cpp(x, knots = knots) dim(crs1) #> [1] 11  3 # Comparison with survPen::crs requires the survPen package: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(survPen) crs2 <- crs(x, knots, intercept = FALSE) range(crs1 - crs2$bs) range(attr(crs1, "pen") - crs2$pen) } # }

what: Fit a penalized general hazard model — fit_genhaz
title: Fit a penalized general hazard model — fit_genhaz
text: High-level interface fitting penalised cubic regression spline general hazard (GH) model. Wraps genhaz_work() automatic knot placement, design matrix construction, optional profiling smoothing parameter lambda via modified LCV criterion.

title: Fit a penalized general hazard model — fit_genhaz
code: fit_genhaz(   surv,   formula,   data,   n_knots = 8,   lambda = 0,   model_type = "GH",   control = list(trace = 0, x.tol = 1e-12, rel.tol = 1e-12, max_iter = 100),   profile = FALSE,   lcv_method = c("full", "approx", "optimize"),   lambda_surv = FALSE,   init = NULL,   interval = c(0, 10),   nquad = 25,   tol_LCV = 0.001,   knots = NULL,   reKnot = 1,   limit_to_95 = TRUE,   timeIt = FALSE )

title: Fit a penalized general hazard model — fit_genhaz
text: surv Surv object (survival package). Right- censored (Surv(time, event)), left-truncated right-censored (Surv(start, stop, event)), interval-censored (Surv(t1, t2, type = "interval2")) data supported. formula one-sided formula specifying covariate terms, e.g. ~ x + z. intercept added internally; include one . data data frame containing variables referenced formula. n_knots Total number knots baseline log-hazard spline, including two boundary knots. Default 8. lambda Initial (fixed) smoothing parameter. Ignored profile = TRUE. Default 0. model_type Character vector length 1 ncol(X) selecting model class per covariate: "GH" (general hazard, default), "PH" (proportional hazards), "AFT" (accelerated failure time), "AH" (additive hazards). control List optimisation controls nlminb(): trace, x.tol, rel.tol, max_iter. profile Logical. TRUE, optimise lambda via LCV returning final fit. Default FALSE. lcv_method Character string passed genhaz_work() profile = TRUE. Selects strategy optimising log(lambda): "full" (default) uses full third-derivative LCV gradient, "approx" uses faster first-order approximation, "optimize" minimises LCV directly without gradient information. Ignored profile = FALSE. lambda_surv Logical. TRUE, initialise lambda survPen proportional hazard fit. Requires survPen installed. Default FALSE. init Optional numeric vector starting values. NULL, zeros used. interval Length-2 numeric vector giving search interval log(lambda) LCV optimisation. Default c(0, 10). nquad Number Gauss-Legendre quadrature points. Default 25. tol_LCV Convergence tolerance LCV optimisation. Default 1e-3. knots Optional numeric vector knot positions log-time scale. NULL (default), knots placed automatically using knot_pattern(). reKnot Non-negative integer. initial fit, re-position knots abs(reKnot) times using AFT-adjusted times refit. Default 1 (one re-knot pass). Set 0 skip. limit_to_95 Logical passed knot_pattern(). Default TRUE. timeIt Logical. TRUE, print wall-clock fitting time. Default FALSE.

title: Fit a penalized general hazard model — fit_genhaz
text: list class "genhaz_fit" containing (among others): par Named vector parameter estimates. se Standard errors. z Wald z-statistics. p_values Two-sided p-values. var Estimated covariance matrix par. AIC Akaike information criterion. edf Effective degrees freedom. lambda Smoothing parameter used. knots Knot vector log-time scale. Z Spline projection matrix.

title: Fit a penalized general hazard model — fit_genhaz
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(survival) set.seed(42) dat <- sim_scenario(scenario = 1, beta1 = 0.5, beta2 = 0.5, n = 300) fit <- fit_genhaz(Surv(dat$time, dat$event), ~ X, data = dat,                   model_type = "GH", profile = TRUE, n_knots = 6,                   tol_LCV = 0.01) fit$par fit$se } # }

title: Pre-computed melanoma GH model fit — fit_melanoma
code: fit_melanoma

title: Pre-computed melanoma GH model fit — fit_melanoma
text: genhaz_fit object; see fit_genhaz.

title: Pre-computed melanoma GH model fit — fit_melanoma
text: biostat3::melanoma; fit produced data-raw/prepare_melanoma_fit.R.

what: genhaz: General Hazard Models for Survival Analysis — genhaz-package
title: genhaz: General Hazard Models for Survival Analysis — genhaz-package
text: Fits penalized cubic spline generalized hazards (GH) models survival data, encompassing proportional hazards (PH), accelerated failure time (AFT), additive hazards (AH), full GH specification. Smoothing parameter selection via modified leave-one-cross-validation (LCV) criterion. Post-estimation inference includes pointwise delta-method confidence intervals hazard, survival, cumulative hazard, restricted mean survival time (RMST), -group differences. Also provides mixture Weibull utilities data generation simulation studies.

title: genhaz: General Hazard Models for Survival Analysis — genhaz-package
text: Maintainer: Aaron Jehle aaron.jehle@mail.de Authors: Mark Clements mark.clements@ki.se

title: General hazard model workhorse — genhaz_work
text: Calculates various quantities general hazard (GH) model depending res argument: negative log-likelihood gradient/Hessian, cumulative hazard H derivatives, effective degrees freedom, LCV criterion, fully fitted model object. called directly users; use fit_genhaz() post() instead.

title: General hazard model workhorse — genhaz_work
code: genhaz_work(   theta,   time,   X,   knots,   Z,   event = NULL,   lambda = 1,   res = c("log_h", "gradient_log_h", "hessian_log_h", "dlogh_dt", "dh_dt", "h",     "gradient_h", "hessian_h", "H", "gradient_H", "hessian_H", "negll", "negll_upen",     "scores", "gradient_negll", "hessian_negll", "hessian_negll_upen", "basis", "edf",     "LCV", "dLCV", "dLCV_full", "fit", "fit_LCV"),   model_type = "GH",   time2 = NULL,   cens_type = "rc",   gaussified = TRUE,   nquad = 25,   control = list(trace = 0, x.tol = 1e-12, rel.tol = 1e-12, max_iter = 100),   lcv_method = c("full", "approx", "optimize"),   interval = c(0, 13),   tol_LCV = 0.001,   ... )

title: General hazard model workhorse — genhaz_work
text: theta Numeric vector parameters: intercept, spline coefficients, beta1 (AFT-style effects) beta2 (PH-style effects). time Numeric vector observed (evaluation) times. X Numeric matrix covariates (rows = observations). knots Numeric vector knot positions log-time scale, including boundary knots. Z Projection matrix spline basis (smf_cpp()). event Integer event indicator vector (1 = event, 0 = censored). Required fitting. lambda Non-negative smoothing parameter spline penalty. res Character string selecting quantity return. One "log_h", "gradient_log_h", "hessian_log_h", "dlogh_dt" (derivative log-hazard w.r.t. time), "dh_dt" (derivative hazard w.r.t. time), "h", "gradient_h", "hessian_h", "H", "gradient_H", "hessian_H", "negll", "negll_upen", "scores", "gradient_negll", "hessian_negll", "hessian_negll_upen", "basis", "edf", "LCV", "dLCV", "dLCV_full", "fit", "fit_LCV". last two fit model: "fit" fixed lambda, "fit_LCV" profiling lambda via LCV. "dLCV" returns first-order analytical derivative LCV w.r.t. log(lambda) (evaluated MLE given lambda). "dLCV_full" returns derivative including third-derivative correction term unpenalised Hessian's dependence lambda (supports "rc" "lt_rc"; falls back "dLCV" "ic"). model_type Character vector specifying model type covariate: "GH" (general hazard), "PH" (proportional hazards, beta1 = 0), "AFT" (accelerated failure time, beta2 = 0), "AH" (additive hazards, beta1 = -beta2). length 1, recycled covariates. time2 Numeric vector second time variable. Used truncation time cens_type = "lt_rc", right interval endpoint cens_type = "ic". cens_type Censoring type: "rc" (right-censoring, default), "lt_rc" (left-truncation + right-censoring), "ic" (interval censoring). gaussified Logical; use Gauss-Legendre quadrature integrating cumulative hazard? Default TRUE. Setting FALSE requires rstpm2 installed. nquad Number quadrature points gaussified = TRUE. Default 25. control List optimisation control parameters passed nlminb(): trace, x.tol, rel.tol, max_iter. lcv_method Character string. res = "fit_LCV", selects strategy optimising log(lambda). One : "full" (default) Root-find full LCV gradient including third-derivative correction (dLCV_full). accurate. "approx" Root-find first-order LCV gradient (dLCV), ignores dependence Hessian lambda. Faster "full". "optimize" Directly minimise LCV without gradient information via optimize(). Equivalent original behaviour analytic gradients added. Ignored res values falls back lcv_gradient cens_type = "ic". interval Length-2 numeric vector giving search interval log(lambda) LCV optimisation. Default c(0, 13). tol_LCV Convergence tolerance LCV optimisation. Default 1e-3. ... Additional arguments (currently unused).

title: General hazard model workhorse — genhaz_work
text: Depends res. res = "fit" "fit_LCV", returns list components par, se, z, p_values, AIC, edf, lambda, var, fitting details.

what: Compute knot positions from event times — knot_pattern
title: Compute knot positions from event times — knot_pattern
text: Places n_knots equally spaced (probability) quantiles log-event-times. Boundary knots placed 5 % , optionally, 95 % 100 % quantile event-time distribution.

title: Compute knot positions from event times — knot_pattern
code: knot_pattern(time, event, n_knots, limit_to_95 = TRUE)

title: Compute knot positions from event times — knot_pattern
text: time Numeric vector observed times. event Integer vector event indicators (1 = event, 0 = censored), length time. n_knots Total number knots, including two boundary knots. limit_to_95 Logical. TRUE (default) upper boundary knot placed 95 % quantile; FALSE placed maximum observed event time.

title: Compute knot positions from event times — knot_pattern
text: Named numeric vector length n_knots knot positions log-time scale, required genhaz_work() fit_genhaz().

title: Compute knot positions from event times — knot_pattern

what: Mixture Weibull GH model quantities — mixWeib
title: Mixture Weibull GH model quantities — mixWeib
text: Evaluates survival function ("S"), cumulative hazard ("H"), hazard ("h") two-component mixture Weibull GH model.

title: Mixture Weibull GH model quantities — mixWeib
code: mixWeib(res = "S", t, p, lambda1, lambda2, gamma1, gamma2, X, beta1, beta2)

title: Mixture Weibull GH model quantities — mixWeib
text: res Character: "S", "H", "h". t Numeric vector time points. p Mixture proportion (weight first component), (0, 1). lambda1, lambda2 Scale parameters two Weibull components. gamma1, gamma2 Shape parameters two Weibull components. X Numeric covariate value (scalar vector). beta1 AFT-type covariate coefficient. beta2 PH-type covariate coefficient.

title: Mixture Weibull GH model quantities — mixWeib
text: Numeric vector requested quantity.

title: Mixture Weibull GH model quantities — mixWeib

what: Mixture Weibull model for a named simulation scenario — mixWeibSc
title: Mixture Weibull model for a named simulation scenario — mixWeibSc
text: Convenience wrapper around mixWeib() three simulation scenarios used accompanying study: Scenario 1 Bathtub-like hazard (p=0.8, lambda1=lambda2=0.1, gamma1=3, gamma2=1.6). Scenario 2 Unimodal hazard (p=0.5, lambda1=lambda2=1, gamma1=1.5, gamma2=0.5). Scenario 3 Bimodal hazard (p=0.26, lambda1=0.02, lambda2=0.5, gamma1=3, gamma2=0.7).

title: Mixture Weibull model for a named simulation scenario — mixWeibSc
code: mixWeibSc(scenario, res = "S", t, X = 0, beta1 = 0, beta2 = 0)

title: Mixture Weibull model for a named simulation scenario — mixWeibSc
text: scenario Integer (1, 2, 3) selecting scenario. res Character: "S", "H", "h". t Numeric vector time points. X Covariate value. Default 0. beta1 AFT coefficient. Default 0. beta2 PH coefficient. Default 0.

title: Mixture Weibull model for a named simulation scenario — mixWeibSc
text: Numeric vector.

title: Mixture Weibull model for a named simulation scenario — mixWeibSc
code: t <- seq(0.01, 5, length.out = 100) h1 <- mixWeibSc(1, "h", t, X = 1, beta1 = 0.5, beta2 = 0.5)

what: Mixture Weibull survival, cumulative hazard, and hazard functions — mixture_weibull
title: Mixture Weibull survival, cumulative hazard, and hazard functions — mixture_weibull
text: two-component mixture Weibull model general hazard (GH) parametrisation. functions primarily intended generating synthetic survival data simulation studies. survival function $$S(t \mid X) = \bigl[p \exp(-\lambda_1 t^{\gamma_1} e^{X \beta_1 \gamma_1}) +   (1-p)\exp(-\lambda_2 t^{\gamma_2} e^{X \beta_1 \gamma_2})\bigr]^{\exp(\beta_2 X)}$$

what: Plot estimated curves from a fitted GH model — plot.genhaz_fit
title: Plot estimated curves from a fitted GH model — plot.genhaz_fit
text: Calls predict.genhaz_fit() passes result plot.genhaz_pred(). Supports nine prediction types; ylim automatically derived full range CI bands line clipped.

title: Plot estimated curves from a fitted GH model — plot.genhaz_fit
code: # S3 method for class 'genhaz_fit' plot(   x,   newdata,   times,   type = c("hazard", "survival", "cumhaz", "rmst", "surv_diff", "rmst_diff",     "hazard_ratio", "time_ratio", "acc_factor"),   interval = c("none", "confidence"),   level = 0.95,   tau_max = NULL,   ... )

title: Plot estimated curves from a fitted GH model — plot.genhaz_fit
text: x "genhaz_fit" object fit_genhaz(). newdata data.frame covariate values. One row per group. Row names become legend labels. times Numeric vector evaluation time points. type Quantity plot. One "hazard" (default), "survival", "cumhaz", "rmst", "surv_diff", "rmst_diff", "hazard_ratio", "time_ratio", "acc_factor". last five require exactly two rows newdata. interval Passed predict.genhaz_fit(). "none" (default) plots point estimate; "confidence" computes overlays delta-method confidence bands. level Confidence level interval = "confidence". Default 0.95. tau_max Passed predict.genhaz_fit(); upper bound time warp used "time_ratio" "acc_factor". Default NULL (model support). ... Additional arguments passed plot.genhaz_pred() graphics::plot() (e.g. col, lty, xlab, main, xlim).

title: Plot estimated curves from a fitted GH model — plot.genhaz_fit
text: Invisibly returns "genhaz_pred" object predict.genhaz_fit().

title: Plot estimated curves from a fitted GH model — plot.genhaz_fit
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ nd <- data.frame(X = c(0, 1)) rownames(nd) <- c("X = 0", "X = 1") t_grid <- seq(0.01, 8, length.out = 300)  plot(fit, newdata = nd, times = t_grid) plot(fit, newdata = nd, times = t_grid, type = "survival",      col = c("steelblue", "firebrick")) plot(fit, newdata = nd, times = t_grid, type = "hazard_ratio",      col = "purple") } # }

title: Plot a genhaz prediction — plot.genhaz_pred
text: Plots output predict.genhaz_fit() — nine prediction types. distinct covariate pattern becomes coloured line; confidence bands drawn dashed lines colour. ylim always derived full range CI bands plus point estimate, nothing clipped. Axis labels title set automatically prediction type can overridden via ylab / main.

title: Plot a genhaz prediction — plot.genhaz_pred
code: # S3 method for class 'genhaz_pred' plot(   x,   col = NULL,   lty = 1,   xlab = "Time",   ylab = NULL,   main = NULL,   legend = TRUE,   ci = TRUE,   ylim = NULL,   ... )

title: Plot a genhaz prediction — plot.genhaz_pred
text: x "genhaz_pred" object returned predict.genhaz_fit(). col Colour vector (recycled groups). Defaults 2, 3, 4, ... (R's standard colour sequence, skipping black). lty Line type point-estimate curves. Default 1. xlab X-axis label. Default "Time". ylab Y-axis label. Derived prediction type NULL. main Plot title. Derived prediction type NULL. legend Logical; draw legend multiple groups present? Default TRUE. ci Logical; overlay dashed confidence band lines? Default TRUE. ylim Numeric length-2 y-axis limits. NULL (default), derived full range point estimate CI bands nothing clipped. ... Additional graphical parameters passed graphics::plot().

title: Plot a genhaz prediction — plot.genhaz_pred
text: Invisibly returns x.

title: Plot a genhaz prediction — plot.genhaz_pred
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ nd <- data.frame(X = c(0, 1)) rownames(nd) <- c("X = 0", "X = 1") t_grid <- seq(0.01, 8, length.out = 200)  pred <- predict(fit, newdata = nd, times = t_grid, type = "hazard_ratio") plot(pred, col = "purple") abline(h = 1, lty = 2) } # }

what: Post-estimation from a fitted general hazard model — post
title: Post-estimation from a fitted general hazard model — post
text: Evaluates model quantities (hazard, cumulative hazard, survival, gradients, etc.) arbitrary covariate patterns time points, using parameters fitted model object.

title: Post-estimation from a fitted general hazard model — post
code: post(fit, X, time, res, event = NULL)

title: Post-estimation from a fitted general hazard model — post
text: fit fitted model object returned fit_genhaz(). X Covariate vector matrix prediction. vector, treated single covariate pattern replicated across time points. single-row matrix length(time) > 1, row replicated. NULL, covariates set zero. time Numeric vector time points evaluate quantity. res Character string; quantity return. Supports non-fitting options genhaz_work(): "h", "H", "log_h", "gradient_H", "gradient_h", etc. event Optional event indicator vector, required res values (e.g. "scores").

title: Post-estimation from a fitted general hazard model — post
text: Numeric vector matrix requested quantity evaluated (time, X) combination.

title: Post-estimation from a fitted general hazard model — post
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(survival) set.seed(42) dat <- sim_scenario(1, beta1 = 0.5, beta2 = 0.5, n = 300) fit <- fit_genhaz(Surv(dat$time, dat$event), ~X, data = dat,                   n_knots = 6, profile = TRUE, tol_LCV = 0.01)  t_grid <- seq(0.01, 8, length.out = 200) h_est  <- post(fit, X = 0, time = t_grid, res = "h") plot(t_grid, h_est, type = "l", xlab = "Time", ylab = "Hazard") } # }

what: Predict from a fitted GH model — predict.genhaz_fit
title: Predict from a fitted GH model — predict.genhaz_fit
text: Evaluates fitted model one covariate patterns time grid, returning pointwise estimates delta-method confidence intervals. requested quantity computed.

title: Predict from a fitted GH model — predict.genhaz_fit
code: # S3 method for class 'genhaz_fit' predict(   object,   newdata,   times,   type = c("hazard", "survival", "cumhaz", "rmst", "surv_diff", "rmst_diff",     "hazard_ratio", "time_ratio", "acc_factor"),   interval = c("none", "confidence"),   level = 0.95,   tau_max = NULL,   ... )

title: Predict from a fitted GH model — predict.genhaz_fit
text: object "genhaz_fit" object fit_genhaz(). newdata data.frame covariate values. row defines one covariate pattern; row names used group labels output. Must contain variables referenced model formula. two-group comparison types, exactly two rows required: row 1 exposed group (group 1) row 2 unexposed/baseline group (group 0). times Numeric vector time points. "rmst" "rmst_diff", restriction times tau RMST(tau) = integral_0^tau S(t) dt evaluated. type Quantity predict: "hazard" (default), "survival", "cumhaz" (cumulative hazard), "rmst" (restricted mean survival time), "surv_diff" (survival difference S1−S0), "rmst_diff" (RMST difference), "hazard_ratio" (h1/h0), "time_ratio" (tau/t S0(tau) = S1(t): baseline time tau baseline group reaches comparison group's survival t), "acc_factor" (time-varying acceleration effect log scale, f(t) = log tau'(t) = log h1(t) - log h0(tau), tau defined H0(tau) = H1(t); satisfies S(t | x1) = S0(integral_0^t exp(f(u) X) du), .e. tau(t) = integral_0^t exp(f(u) X) du instantaneous rate exp(f(t) X) = tau'(t), f(t) = beta1 constant-AFT limit directly comparable beta1). last five require exactly two rows newdata. interval Type interval return, following stats::predict.lm(). "none" (default) computes point estimate (skipping delta-method gradients root-solving overhead require) returns lower/upper columns NA; "confidence" adds delta-method Wald confidence interval. level Confidence level interval = "confidence". Default 0.95. tau_max Upper bound equal-cumulative-hazard time tau solved "time_ratio" "acc_factor". Defaults model support exp(max(object$knots)) (largest knot, beyond spline pure linear extrapolation). baseline group reach comparison group's cumulative hazard within [0, tau_max], warp identified corresponding rows returned NA warning; pass larger value allow extrapolation. Ignored types. ... Currently unused.

title: Predict from a fitted GH model — predict.genhaz_fit
text: data.frame class c("genhaz_pred", "data.frame") columns pattern, time, estimate, lower, upper (latter two NA interval = "none"). pred_type attribute records quantity computed; plot() uses set axis labels automatically. per-group types rows grouped covariate pattern; two-group types single block labelled "group1 - group0" (exposed - unexposed) returned.

title: Predict from a fitted GH model — predict.genhaz_fit
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ t_grid <- seq(0.01, 8, length.out = 200) nd <- data.frame(X = c(1, 0)) rownames(nd) <- c("Exposed", "Unexposed")  predict(fit, newdata = nd, times = t_grid, type = "survival",         interval = "confidence") predict(fit, newdata = nd, times = c(1, 2, 5), type = "rmst") predict(fit, newdata = nd, times = t_grid, type = "surv_diff") predict(fit, newdata = nd, times = c(1, 2, 5), type = "rmst_diff") predict(fit, newdata = nd, times = t_grid, type = "hazard_ratio") predict(fit, newdata = nd, times = t_grid, type = "time_ratio") predict(fit, newdata = nd, times = t_grid, type = "acc_factor") } # }

what: Simulate data from a mixture Weibull GH model — sim_mix_weib_gh
title: Simulate data from a mixture Weibull GH model — sim_mix_weib_gh
text: Simulates right-censored survival data two-component mixture Weibull GH model binary covariate X ~ Bernoulli(0.5). Censoring times drawn Uniform(0, tmax).

title: Simulate data from a mixture Weibull GH model — sim_mix_weib_gh
code: sim_mix_weib_gh(   n,   p,   lambda1,   lambda2,   gamma1,   gamma2,   beta1,   beta2,   tmax = 10 )

title: Simulate data from a mixture Weibull GH model — sim_mix_weib_gh
text: n Sample size. p Mixture proportion. lambda1, lambda2 Weibull scale parameters. gamma1, gamma2 Weibull shape parameters. beta1 AFT coefficient X. beta2 PH coefficient X. tmax Administrative censoring time (uniform censoring upper bound).

title: Simulate data from a mixture Weibull GH model — sim_mix_weib_gh
text: Data frame columns time, X, event, T_true.

title: Simulate data from a mixture Weibull GH model — sim_mix_weib_gh

what: Simulate data for a named scenario — sim_scenario
title: Simulate data for a named scenario — sim_scenario
text: Convenience wrapper around sim_mix_weib_gh() using three pre-defined simulation scenarios (see mixWeibSc()).

title: Simulate data for a named scenario — sim_scenario
code: sim_scenario(scenario, beta1, beta2, n = 1000, tmax = 10)

title: Simulate data for a named scenario — sim_scenario
text: scenario Integer (1, 2, 3). beta1 AFT coefficient. beta2 PH coefficient. n Sample size. tmax Administrative censoring time.

title: Simulate data for a named scenario — sim_scenario
text: Data frame columns time, X, event, T_true.

title: Simulate data for a named scenario — sim_scenario
code: set.seed(1) dat <- sim_scenario(1, beta1 = 0.5, beta2 = 0.5, n = 500) table(dat$event) #>  #>   0   1  #>  94 406

what: C++ reimplementation of smf, including derivatives. — smf_cpp
title: C++ reimplementation of smf, including derivatives. — smf_cpp
text: re-implementation uses algorithm uses different defaults. Note inclusion intercept crs_cpp, intercept uses projection based QR decomposition. changes defaults: function assumes intercept false (per splines::ns); knots specified, function defaults using x rather unique(x) quantiles. survPen implementation smf assumes intercept unless variable. variable x2 implemented interaction, smf_cpp(x,knots,intercept=TRUE):x2; see examples.

title: C++ reimplementation of smf, including derivatives. — smf_cpp
code: smf_cpp(   x,   df = 10L,   knots = NULL,   intercept = FALSE,   derivs = 0L,   Z = NULL,   survPen_compatible = FALSE )

title: C++ reimplementation of smf, including derivatives. — smf_cpp
text: x vec values provide df non-negative integer degrees freedom (ignored knots specified) knots vec knots (defaults NULL) intercept bool whether include intercept derivs non-negative integer derivative (default 0) Z optional pre-computed QR projection matrix (arma::mat). provided, overrides internally computed centering matrix. survPen_compatible bool whether use unique(x) determining knots knots specified

title: C++ reimplementation of smf, including derivatives. — smf_cpp
text: Rcpp::NumericMatrix attributes class="smf_cpp", pen penalty matrix, knots knots, derivs order derivatives, intercept bool/logical whether intercept included. intercept, also Z attribute QR-based projection matrix.

title: C++ reimplementation of smf, including derivatives. — smf_cpp
code: x     <- seq(0, 1, length.out = 11) knots <- c(1, 3, 5, 7) / 10 smf1  <- smf_cpp(x, knots = knots) dim(smf1) #> [1] 11  3 # Check that Z attribute is available without intercept: !is.null(attr(smf1, "Z")) #> [1] TRUE  ## Derivative check via finite differences (knots must be explicit) smfD <- function(t, knots, eps = 1e-5) {   (smf_cpp(t + eps, knots = knots) - smf_cpp(t - eps, knots = knots)) / (2 * eps) } x2 <- seq(0, 1, length.out = 101) max(abs(smf_cpp(x2, knots = knots, derivs = 1) - smfD(x2, knots = knots))) #> [1] 11.83606  ## Comparison with survPen::smooth.cons (requires survPen): if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(survPen) smf2 <- smooth.cons("x", knots = list(knots), df = 4, option = "smf",                     data.spec = data.frame(x = x), name = "smf(time)") range(smf1 - smf2$X) range(attr(smf1, "pen") - smf2$pen[[1]]) } # }

what: Summarise a fitted GH model — summary.genhaz_fit
title: Summarise a fitted GH model — summary.genhaz_fit
text: Returns "summary.genhaz_fit" object containing model metadata covariate coefficient table Wald confidence intervals exponentiated estimates.

title: Summarise a fitted GH model — summary.genhaz_fit
text: object "genhaz_fit" object fit_genhaz(). alpha Significance level confidence intervals. Default 0.05. ... Currently unused.

title: Summarise a fitted GH model — summary.genhaz_fit
text: object class "summary.genhaz_fit" (printed print.summary.genhaz_fit()).

title: Summarise a fitted GH model — summary.genhaz_fit
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ summary(fit) } # }

what: Time-varying acceleration factor between two covariate patterns — translate_time
title: Time-varying acceleration factor between two covariate patterns — translate_time
text: two covariate patterns covariate0 covariate1, computes time-varying acceleration factor phi(t) = h0(t) / h1(tau(t)), tau defined H0(t) = H1(tau(t)) (equal cumulative-hazard time mapping). Requires rstpm2 vectorised root-finding.

title: Time-varying acceleration factor between two covariate patterns — translate_time
code: translate_time(fit, covariate0, covariate1, time)

title: Time-varying acceleration factor between two covariate patterns — translate_time
text: fit fitted model object fit_genhaz(). covariate0 Numeric vector: covariate pattern reference group. covariate1 Numeric vector: covariate pattern comparison group. time Numeric vector evaluation time points.

title: Time-varying acceleration factor between two covariate patterns — translate_time
text: Numeric vector phi(t) values.

what: Time-varying tau mapping between two covariate patterns — translate_time2
title: Time-varying tau mapping between two covariate patterns — translate_time2
text: Returns tau(t), defined H0(t) = H1(tau(t)), .e. time comparison group accumulated cumulative hazard reference group time t. Requires rstpm2.

title: Time-varying tau mapping between two covariate patterns — translate_time2
code: translate_time2(fit, covariate0, covariate1, time)

title: Time-varying tau mapping between two covariate patterns — translate_time2
text: fit fitted model object fit_genhaz(). covariate0 Numeric vector: covariate pattern reference group. covariate1 Numeric vector: covariate pattern comparison group. time Numeric vector evaluation time points.

title: Time-varying tau mapping between two covariate patterns — translate_time2
text: Data frame columns time tvta (tau values).
